wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Barcode Adventure project overview. Explain the project's purpose as a gamified barcode scanning application that turns product discovery into an engaging adventure. Document the core concept of combining real-time barcode scanning with RPG-style gamification elements like XP, levels, achievements, and daily missions. Describe the target audience and primary use cases. Include the technology stack overview with Next.js 16, TypeScript, Prisma ORM, and modern web technologies. Provide a high-level architecture diagram showing the relationship between frontend components, backend API routes, database layer, and gamification engine. Explain how the application transforms mundane barcode scanning into an entertaining user experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: Scan API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Scan API endpoint. Document the POST /api/scan endpoint that processes barcode scans and returns product information. Detail the request schema with barcodeNumber and deviceType parameters, validation rules, and error handling. Explain the response format including success flags, product data (when found), and scan log information. Include practical examples showing successful scans, not found scenarios, and error responses. Document the database operations performed (product lookup and scan log creation) and provide troubleshooting guidance for common scanning issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system overview documentation for Barcode Adventure. Explain the overall system architecture, high-level design patterns, and major architectural decisions. Document the Clean Architecture implementation with clear separation between presentation, business logic, and data layers. Describe the technology stack integration including Next.js App Router, React components, Prisma ORM, and Zustand state management. Include system boundaries, main data flows, and how different architectural layers interact. Provide architectural diagrams showing component relationships and explain the rationale behind key design choices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Barcode Scanning System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the barcode scanning system component. Document the real-time camera integration using react-zxing, multi-format barcode detection (EAN-13, UPC-A, Code-128, Code-39), and device compatibility handling. Explain the scanning workflow from camera initialization to result processing, including error handling for camera permissions and device switching. Cover the integration with the backend scan API, result validation, and user feedback mechanisms. Include implementation details for camera constraints, performance optimizations, and accessibility features. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common scanning issues and browser compatibility considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: XP System & Leveling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the XP calculation and leveling system. Explain the XP award mechanisms for different actions (scanning, registering products), bonus multipliers, and progression curves. Document the level-up thresholds, experience requirements, and scaling algorithms. Include formulas used for XP calculations, level progression mathematics, and reward distribution. Provide examples of XP earned for various activities and demonstrate how the system balances challenge and progression. Address performance considerations for real-time XP updates and user feedback mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the Barcode Adventure project. Include detailed prerequisites covering Node.js version requirements, database setup (PostgreSQL), and development environment configuration. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including dependency installation, database initialization, and environment variable configuration. Document the development server startup process and initial project structure walkthrough. Include first-time user scenarios showing how to access the landing page, navigate to the scanner interface, and perform their first barcode scan. Provide troubleshooting tips for common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Include examples of basic commands and expected outputs during the setup process.

wiki_catalogs.name: Products API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the Products API covering both collection and individual resource endpoints. Document GET /api/products for retrieving all products with pagination and filtering, POST /api/products for creating new products, and GET /api/products/[barcode] for retrieving specific products. Detail request/response schemas, validation rules, and error handling for each endpoint. Include examples of product data structures, search parameters, and CRUD operations. Document authentication requirements, rate limiting, and integration patterns for product management workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend architecture documentation for Barcode Adventure. Document the Next.js App Router implementation with file-based routing patterns and dynamic routes. Explain the component hierarchy from root layout through page components to reusable UI components. Detail the component composition patterns, prop drilling strategies, and component lifecycle management. Include the styling architecture using Tailwind CSS and custom component library. Document the responsive design patterns, accessibility implementation, and cross-browser compatibility considerations. Explain how the frontend handles real-time updates, animations, and user interactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Gamification Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the gamification engine that drives user engagement through XP systems, achievements, and daily missions. Detail the game mechanics including XP calculation algorithms, level progression curves, achievement unlock criteria, and mission generation logic. Explain the integration between player state management and gamification triggers. Document the visual feedback systems including XP popups, achievement notifications, and progress indicators. Cover the game configuration system that allows for customizable difficulty and reward structures. Include implementation details for streak tracking, bonus multipliers, and social features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Achievement System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the achievement system architecture and implementation. Detail the predefined achievement definitions, unlock criteria, and trigger conditions. Explain the achievement evaluation logic, state management for unlocked achievements, and popup notification system. Document achievement categories (scanning milestones, registration achievements, level-based unlocks, streak achievements) and their respective thresholds. Include examples of achievement triggers, timing considerations for unlock notifications, and integration with the player state management system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture & Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Barcode Adventure system. Describe the high-level design patterns including Clean Architecture with separation of concerns across presentation, business logic, and data layers. Document the Next.js App Router architecture and file-based routing patterns. Explain the component hierarchy from presentation layer (React components) through business logic (game engine) to data access (Prisma ORM). Detail the state management architecture using Zustand for reactive player state. Include system boundaries, data flows between components, and integration patterns with external services like camera APIs and database systems. Document technical decisions, architectural trade-offs, and scalability considerations. Provide component interaction diagrams and explain how different architectural layers communicate.

wiki_catalogs.name: History API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the History API endpoint. Document the GET /api/history endpoint for retrieving scan history with filtering, sorting, and pagination capabilities. Detail query parameters for date ranges, product filters, and device types. Explain response formats including scan log data, product associations, and timestamps. Include examples of common query patterns, bulk operations, and export scenarios. Document performance considerations for large datasets and provide guidance on efficient querying strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend API Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend API architecture documentation for Barcode Adventure. Document the Next.js App Router API routes implementation with proper HTTP method handling and request/response patterns. Explain the API endpoint organization, parameter validation, and error handling strategies. Detail the data flow from API routes through business logic to database operations. Include authentication patterns, CORS configuration, and security considerations. Document the API versioning strategy, rate limiting, and performance optimization techniques. Explain how the backend handles concurrent requests, transaction management, and data consistency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the product management system that handles product data entry, validation, and retrieval. Document the product form interface with webcam capture integration for image uploads, barcode validation, and category management. Explain the product CRUD operations including creation, updates, and deletion workflows. Cover the integration with the barcode scanning system for automatic product lookup and the fallback manual entry process. Detail the product search and filtering capabilities, image handling, and data validation rules. Include implementation specifics for product categorization, duplicate detection, and bulk operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Daily Missions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the daily mission generation and evaluation system. Explain the mission template system, randomization algorithms using date-based seeding, and deterministic mission selection. Document mission types including scanning targets, category-specific challenges, time-based missions, and registration goals. Detail the mission evaluation logic, progress tracking, and completion rewards. Include examples of mission templates, evaluator functions, and the hash-based selection algorithm. Address mission reset mechanisms, player progress persistence, and UI integration for mission display and tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core features of Barcode Adventure. Cover the primary functionality areas including real-time barcode scanning system, gamification engine with XP and achievements, product management capabilities, and user interface components. Explain how each feature integrates with others to create the complete user experience. Document feature workflows, user interactions, and the relationship between frontend components and backend services. Include implementation details for camera integration, barcode detection algorithms, and gamification mechanics. Provide usage examples and common workflows for each major feature area.

wiki_catalogs.name: Statistics API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the Statistics API endpoint. Document the GET /api/statistics endpoint that provides usage analytics and reporting data. Detail available metrics including scan counts, product categories, device usage patterns, and time-based trends. Explain response formats for different aggregation levels and time ranges. Include examples of statistical queries, data visualization preparation, and integration patterns. Document caching strategies, performance optimization, and data freshness considerations for analytics endpoints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Layer Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed data layer architecture documentation for Barcode Adventure. Document the Prisma ORM implementation including schema design, model relationships, and database migration strategies. Explain the data access patterns, query optimization techniques, and caching strategies. Detail the database connection management, transaction handling, and error recovery mechanisms. Include the data seeding process, backup strategies, and production deployment considerations. Document the relationship between Prisma models and database tables, including foreign key constraints and indexing strategies. Explain how the data layer supports the gamification features and maintains data integrity.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Interface Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the reusable UI component library that provides consistent styling and functionality across the application. Document each component including buttons, cards, inputs, dialogs, selects, and tables with their props, variants, and usage patterns. Explain the design system implementation using Tailwind CSS, component composition patterns, and accessibility features. Cover the custom components like PixelCat and their specific use cases. Include guidelines for component customization, theme integration, and responsive design patterns. Document the component hierarchy, prop interfaces, and integration examples with other application features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Streak Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the streak tracking system and daily activity monitoring. Explain streak calculation algorithms, date-based continuity checks, and streak reset conditions. Document streak types (daily scanning streaks, registration streaks) and their respective tracking mechanisms. Detail the integration with scan history, player state management, and achievement triggers for streak milestones. Include examples of streak calculation logic, boundary conditions for streak continuation, and UI representation of streak progress. Address edge cases like timezone handling, offline tracking, and streak recovery mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all REST endpoints in the Barcode Adventure application. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for each endpoint. Include detailed parameter specifications, validation rules, and error handling strategies. Cover the scan API for barcode processing, product API for CRUD operations, history API for scan logs retrieval, statistics API for analytics, and upload API for file handling. Provide practical examples with sample requests and responses. Document rate limiting, security considerations, and integration guidelines. Include troubleshooting information for common API issues and performance optimization tips.

wiki_catalogs.name: Upload API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Upload API endpoint. Document the POST /api/upload endpoint for file uploads, including supported file types, size limitations, and validation rules. Detail multipart form data handling, file processing workflows, and response formats. Include examples of upload scenarios, error handling for invalid files, and integration patterns for product image uploads. Document security considerations, temporary storage management, and cleanup procedures for uploaded files.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive state management architecture documentation for Barcode Adventure. Document the Zustand state management implementation including store creation, action dispatching, and state subscription patterns. Explain the player state architecture, including XP tracking, achievement state, and mission progress. Detail the reactive programming patterns used throughout the application. Include state persistence strategies, hydration from server-side state, and state migration handling. Document the integration between global state and local component state, including performance optimization techniques. Explain how the state management supports the gamification features and real-time updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Barcode Adventure database schema. Detail entity relationships including Product, ScanLog, PlayerState, and Achievement tables with their field definitions, data types, and constraints. Document primary and foreign key relationships, indexing strategies, and database performance considerations. Explain data validation rules, business logic constraints, and referential integrity requirements. Include database schema diagrams and sample data structures. Document Prisma ORM configuration, migration management, and seed data setup. Address data lifecycle, backup strategies, and maintenance procedures. Provide examples of common queries and data access patterns used throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the reusable UI component library in Barcode Adventure. Document each component's visual appearance, behavior, user interaction patterns, and customization options. Include prop definitions, event handlers, and styling customization guidelines. Cover the component hierarchy from primitive elements (buttons, inputs) to composite components (dialogs, cards). Explain responsive design patterns, accessibility compliance, and cross-browser compatibility. Document component composition patterns and integration with the overall design system. Include usage examples with code snippets demonstrating common implementation scenarios. Address animation systems, theme integration, and performance optimization techniques used in the component library.

wiki_catalogs.name: Game Mechanics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the gamification system in Barcode Adventure. Detail the XP calculation algorithms, level progression mechanics, and achievement system design. Explain the daily mission generation logic, reward distribution mechanisms, and streak tracking systems. Document the game engine architecture, state management for player progression, and integration with the main application flow. Include formulas, thresholds, and balancing parameters used in the gamification system. Provide examples of mission types, achievement categories, and progression milestones. Address performance considerations for real-time gamification updates and user experience optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the state management architecture in Barcode Adventure. Explain the Zustand store configuration, action dispatching patterns, and state persistence strategies. Document the player state management system, including XP tracking, level progression, and achievement state. Cover custom hooks for sound management and debounced operations. Detail state synchronization between components, performance optimization techniques, and memory management. Include examples of state updates, subscription patterns, and integration with the game engine. Address state migration strategies, versioning, and debugging approaches for complex state interactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & DevOps
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment documentation for Barcode Adventure. Document the Vercel deployment process, environment variable configuration, and production build optimization. Include database provisioning for PostgreSQL, Prisma migration deployment, and environment-specific configuration management. Cover CI/CD pipeline setup, automated testing integration, and release management processes. Detail monitoring and logging configuration, performance optimization for production, and scaling considerations. Include troubleshooting guides for common deployment issues, rollback procedures, and maintenance tasks. Address security best practices, SSL certificate management, and CDN configuration for optimal performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contributing Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive contributing guidelines for the Barcode Adventure project. Document code style standards, ESLint configuration, and TypeScript best practices. Explain the pull request process, issue reporting guidelines, and feature development workflow. Include guidelines for adding new gamification features, extending the scanner functionality, and contributing UI components. Document testing requirements, code review processes, and documentation standards. Cover the agent skill system contribution guidelines and AI enhancement integration. Provide examples of good commit messages, branch naming conventions, and collaboration workflows. Address community guidelines and maintainer responsibilities.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Barcode Adventure: A Gamified Barcode Scanning Experience

## Project Introduction

**Barcode Adventure** is an innovative web application that transforms everyday barcode scanning into an engaging, gamified experience. The project combines real-time barcode recognition technology with a comprehensive gaming system featuring achievements, daily missions, and progressive leveling mechanics.

### Purpose Statement
The application enables users to scan product barcodes using their device cameras, instantly retrieving product information while earning points, unlocking achievements, and progressing through a rewarding gaming experience.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Gamification**: Implement a comprehensive reward system with XP, levels, achievements, and daily missions
- **Real-time Scanning**: Provide instant barcode recognition through web camera integration
- **Data Collection**: Maintain detailed scan history and product databases
- **User Engagement**: Create an addictive, progressive gaming experience that encourages regular use

### Target Audience
- Everyday consumers interested in product discovery
- Gamers seeking casual, mobile-friendly experiences
- Shoppers looking for product information and comparisons
- Users interested in collecting and tracking their purchasing habits

## Technical Architecture

The application follows a modern Next.js 16 architecture with a clear separation of concerns across frontend, backend, and data layers.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[Next.js Frontend]
    B --> C[API Routes]
    C --> D[Prisma ORM]
    D --> E[(PostgreSQL Database)]
    
    F[Barcode Scanner] --> G[React ZXing]
    H[Game Engine] --> I[Zustand Store]
    I --> J[Local Storage Persistence]
    
    K[Supabase Auth] --> L[User Management]
    M[Vercel Analytics] --> N[Usage Tracking]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Layer**
- Next.js 16 with App Router architecture
- React 19 with TypeScript for type safety
- Tailwind CSS for styling with custom design system
- Motion for animations and GSAP for advanced effects

**Backend Layer**
- RESTful API endpoints for product and scan operations
- Prisma ORM for database abstraction
- PostgreSQL for persistent data storage
- Supabase for authentication and user management

**Gaming System**
- Zustand for state management
- Custom game engine for XP calculation and achievements
- Local storage persistence for player progress

### Design Patterns

The application implements several key design patterns:

- **Repository Pattern**: Prisma client abstracts database operations
- **Observer Pattern**: Zustand store manages reactive state updates
- **Strategy Pattern**: Configurable game mechanics and XP calculations
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic mission generation based on date seeds

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Application Layout** (`src/app/layout.tsx`)
- Global metadata and SEO configuration
- Font loading and theme management
- Analytics integration
- Toast notification system

**Landing Page** (`src/app/page.tsx`)
- Hero section with animated pixel cat characters
- Interactive bento grid showcasing features
- Mouse parallax effects and scroll-triggered animations
- GSAP-powered entrance animations

### Core Modules

**Game Engine** (`src/lib/game-engine.ts`)
- Achievement system with predefined unlock criteria
- Daily mission generation with deterministic algorithms
- XP calculation and level progression mechanics
- Mission evaluation and completion tracking

**Player State Management** (`src/stores/player-store.ts`)
- Persistent player state with localStorage
- Game mode management (Guest vs Arashu)
- XP accumulation and level advancement
- Daily streak tracking and mission progress

**Database Abstraction** (`src/lib/prisma.ts`)
- Lazy initialization for build-time compatibility
- PostgreSQL connection pooling
- Type-safe database operations
- Development vs production environment handling

### Configuration Approach

**Game Configuration** (`src/lib/game-config.ts`)
- Centralized game constants and formulas
- XP point values for different actions
- Cooldown periods for preventing spam
- Animation timing configurations

**Type Definitions** (`src/types/index.ts`)
- Comprehensive TypeScript interfaces
- Category management system
- Color and emoji mapping for UI consistency
- Pagination and API response structures

### External Dependencies

**Core Framework Dependencies**:
- Next.js 16 for SSR and static generation
- React 19 for component-based UI
- Prisma 7.8.0 for database ORM
- Zustand 5.0.14 for state management

**UI and Animation Libraries**:
- React ZXing for barcode scanning
- Motion/GSAP for smooth animations
- Lucide React for consistent iconography
- Tailwind CSS for responsive design

**Development Tools**:
- TypeScript for type safety
- ESLint for code quality
- PostCSS for CSS processing
- Vercel for deployment platform

### Integration Points

**Barcode Recognition** (`src/components/scanner/barcode-scanner.tsx`)
- Real-time camera feed processing
- Multiple barcode format support (EAN-13, UPC-A, Code-128)
- Device-specific optimizations
- Error handling and user feedback

**API Endpoints** (`src/app/api/`)
- `/api/scan` - Barcode lookup and scan logging
- `/api/products` - Product CRUD operations
- `/api/statistics` - Usage analytics and reporting
- `/api/history` - Scan history retrieval

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant Scanner
    participant API
    participant Database
    participant GameEngine
    
    User->>Scanner: Point camera at barcode
    Scanner->>API: POST /api/scan
    API->>Database: Query product by barcode
    Database-->>API: Product data or null
    API->>Database: Create scan log
    API-->>Scanner: Scan result
    Scanner->>GameEngine: Update player state
    GameEngine-->>Scanner: XP and achievements
    Scanner-->>User: Display results
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Barcode Scanning System**
- Real-time camera-based barcode detection
- Support for multiple barcode formats
- Instant product lookup and display
- Error handling for invalid or unknown barcodes

**Gamification Engine**
- Progressive XP system with level progression
- Daily mission generation with randomization
- Achievement unlocking based on milestones
- Streak tracking for daily engagement

**Data Management**
- Comprehensive product database
- Scan history logging
- User preference persistence
- Analytics and statistics tracking

### Implementation Highlights

**Dynamic Mission Generation** (`src/lib/game-engine.ts`)
- Date-seeded mission creation ensures uniqueness
- Configurable mission templates with evaluators
- Automatic XP reward calculation
- Achievement unlock detection

**State Persistence** (`src/stores/player-store.ts`)
- Local storage with automatic synchronization
- Migration support for future schema changes
- Performance optimization with selective updates
- Type-safe state management

**Responsive Design System**
- Mobile-first responsive layout
- Custom animation framework
- Consistent color scheme and typography
- Accessible UI components

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Welcome
    Welcome --> GuestMode: Start Game
    GuestMode --> Scanning: Scan Products
    Scanning --> ResultDisplay: Barcode Found
    Scanning --> ErrorDisplay: Scan Failed
    ResultDisplay --> Scanning: Scan Another
    ErrorDisplay --> Scanning: Try Again
    Scanning --> DailyMissions: Complete Actions
    DailyMissions --> AchievementUnlock: Meet Criteria
    AchievementUnlock --> Scanning: Continue Playing
    Scanning --> Leaderboard: Reach New Level
    Leaderboard --> Scanning: View Progress
```

The Barcode Adventure project represents a sophisticated blend of modern web technologies, gamification principles, and practical utility. Its modular architecture supports both immediate functionality and future expansion, making it an excellent foundation for continued development and feature enhancement.

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [layout.tsx](src/app/layout.tsx)
- [game-engine.ts](src/lib/game-engine.ts)
- [prisma.ts](src/lib/prisma.ts)
- [schema.prisma](prisma/schema.prisma)
- [barcode-scanner.tsx](src/components/scanner/barcode-scanner.tsx)
- [route.ts](src/app/api/scan/route.ts)
- [player-store.ts](src/stores/player-store.ts)
- [game-config.ts](src/lib/game-config.ts)
- [index.ts](src/types/index.ts)
- [route.ts](src/app/api/products/route.ts)
- [page.tsx](src/app/page.tsx)

</blog>
